SemiAnalysis.text:
**New this period:**
- Amazon's AWS margins have increased due to growth in customer spending on Claude through Bedrock, with a 213bp Q/Q increase in EBIT margins.
- AWS has pulled ahead of other cloud service providers (CSPs) in terms of margins, with a differentiated strategy and strong growth in AI revenue.
- Anthropic's ARR has outperformed in 1Q26, contributing to AWS' higher share of 3P model API spend.
- Oracle and Coreweave have disappointed the market with lower-than-expected profits from their cloud arms, while Azure is seeing a downward trend in margins.
- Google Cloud has had a great upwards climb in margins recently, but they do not include training costs from DeepMind in the GCP segment.

**Details:**
**Anthropic Growth and Bedrock Mix Drive AWS Margins Higher While Peers Lag** (https://newsletter.semianalysis.com/p/anthropic-growth-and-bedrock-mix)
- Amazon's AWS margins have inflected upwards due to customer spending growth on Claude through Bedrock, with a 213bp Q/Q increase in EBIT margins.
- AWS has a higher share of 3P model API spend due to its deal structure with Anthropic and Bedrock, contributing to its margin growth.
- Anthropic is a key partner for AWS, with its models being sold through Bedrock, and its ARR has outperformed in 1Q26.
- Other CSPs, including Oracle, Coreweave, and Azure, have seen declining or flat operating margins, while Google Cloud's margins are inflated due to not including training costs from DeepMind.
- Amazon has secured more power than any other cloud provider besides Google, with a strong datacenter footprint and custom chip designs such as Graviton and Trainium.
- The company's token-as-a-service (TaaS) business, including Bedrock, is estimated to be $5.5B in run rate, with 80-90% of customers using Anthropic models.
- Microsoft, in contrast, is heavily IaaS-driven, with a lower mix of TaaS revenue.
- Quantitative figures include:
  - 213bp Q/Q increase in AWS EBIT margins
  - $5.5B estimated run rate for AWS' Bedrock business
  - 37% of total AWS AI revenue coming from Bedrock
  - 80-90% of Bedrock customers using Anthropic models
  - 170% and 60% Q/Q growth in Bedrock revenue in 1Q26 and 4Q25, respectively
- Supply chain and value chain relationships include:
  - AWS as a cloud service provider
  - Anthropic as a key partner for AWS, providing models for Bedrock
  - Google as a competitor to AWS, with its own TaaS business and custom chip designs
  - Microsoft as a competitor to AWS, with a lower mix of TaaS revenue
- Technology transitions include:
  - The shift from IaaS to TaaS in the cloud computing market
  - The increasing importance of custom chip designs, such as Graviton and Trainium, for AI workloads.

Andrej Karpathy \u2013 Blog.text:
**New this period:**
- Andrej Karpathy releases a new art project called microgpt, a single file of 200 lines of pure Python that trains and infers a GPT model.
- The microgpt project simplifies large language models to their bare essentials and is available on GitHub and as a Google Colab notebook.
- The project uses a dataset of 32,000 names and a simple tokenizer that assigns one integer to each unique character in the dataset.

**Details:**
**[microgpt](http://karpathy.github.io/2026/02/12/microgpt/)**
- The core thesis of this piece is the release of microgpt, a simplified large language model implemented in a single Python file.
- Companies mentioned: GitHub, Google (for Google Colab notebook).
- The specific role of GitHub is to host the microgpt source code, and the specific role of Google is to provide the platform for the Google Colab notebook.
- Quantitative figures: 200 lines of code, 32,000 names in the dataset, 26 possible lowercase characters (a-z) in the tokenizer, and 1 special token (BOS) for a total vocabulary size of 27.
- Supply chain or value chain relationships: the project relies on the GitHub platform for hosting the code and the Google Colab notebook for interactive execution.
- Technology transitions: microgpt simplifies large language models by reducing them to their essential components, making it easier to understand and implement these models.
- The project uses a simple tokenizer and autograd engine, which are key components of large language models, and demonstrates their usage in a simplified setting.
- The autograd engine is implemented using a Value class that wraps scalar numbers and tracks how they were computed, allowing for automatic differentiation and gradient computation.
- No specific capital allocation signals or regulatory signals are mentioned in this piece.

Ethan Mollick \u2013 One Useful Thing.text:
**New this period:**
- AI-generated writing is increasingly prevalent, but can lack human meaning and undermine the development of important human tasks like writing.
- Using AI as a default or without thinking can lead to "cognitive surrender" and hinder learning.
- AI can be a useful tool for learning when used properly, such as with guided learning modes.
- The way AI tools are designed can encourage or discourage cognitive surrender.

**Details:**
**Choosing to Stay Human (https://www.oneusefulthing.org/p/choosing-to-stay-human)**
- The author argues that while AI can be a useful tool for writing and learning, over-reliance on it can lead to a lack of human meaning and undermine the development of important human tasks.
- Companies mentioned: 
  - ChatGPT (provider of AI writing tools)
  - Anthropic (provider of AI tools, including Claude)
  - Google (implied, as the developer of Gemini)
  - Boston Consulting Group (user of AI tools)
- Quantitative figures:
  - 0.15 standard deviations (the improvement in student scores when using AI tutoring)
  - 6-9 months (the equivalent of additional schooling achieved through AI tutoring)
  - 758 (the number of consultants participating in an experiment on AI use)
- Supply chain or value chain relationships:
  - ChatGPT provides AI writing tools to users
  - Anthropic provides AI tools, including Claude, to users
  - Boston Consulting Group uses AI tools in its consulting work
- Technology transitions:
  - AI writing tools are replacing human writing in some areas, such as social media posts and academic papers
  - AI tutoring is being used to supplement human teaching
- Competitive positioning:
  - Companies that design AI tools to encourage human thought and effort may have a competitive advantage over those that do not
- Capital allocation signals:
  - Investment in AI research and development is likely to continue, with a focus on creating tools that support human learning and effort
- Regulatory or policy signals:
  - None explicitly mentioned, but the author notes that the defaults being set for AI use will be hard to reverse once a generation of workers and students has built habits around them.

Import AI \u2013 Jack Clark.text:
**New this period:**
- The Cosmos HAI Lab Lecture discusses the rapid advance of AI technology and its implications for individuals and society.
- The Epoch Capabilities Index (ECI) shows the score of different AI models over time on a basket of 40+ distinct benchmarks, indicating significant progress in AI capabilities.
- AI systems have achieved various milestones, including passing the bar exam, winning medals in the International Math Olympiad, co-authoring new mathematical proofs, and finding novel flaws in software.
- The growth rate of AI progress is increasing over time, with companies investing hundreds of billions of dollars in computing facilities to train future AI systems.
- The development of AI raises significant risks and challenges, including the potential for recursive self-improvement and immense change to the economy and society.

Arxiv \u2013 Trading & Market Microstruc.text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers analyze stochastic models of dynamic groundwater markets with stochastic groundwater allocations and opportunities for intertemporal transfer through rights banking.
- An empirical analysis reveals unexpected features of asset volumes connected to traders' belief and risk aversion, proposing a trading strategy model in the optimal Merton framework.
- Privacy-preserving cryptocurrency exchanges alter the pricing mechanism's observation of order flow, deriving the unique linear Kyle equilibrium under noise-perturbed order-flow observation.
- The Glosten-Milgrom sequential-trading model is analyzed when the market maker observes the trade direction perturbed by a binary flip channel, deriving a closed-form bid-ask spread and welfare decomposition.
- The privacy subsidy concept is extended from continuous Gaussian to discrete two-state microstructure, demonstrating robustness across classical models.
- The closed-form privacy-subsidy result is extended from the single-period Kyle model to continuous-time, analyzing a committed Bayesian automated market maker under noise-perturbed order-flow observation.

**Details:**
**Multiperiod Groundwater Markets** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26363)
- The paper constructs and analyzes stochastic models of dynamic groundwater markets with multiple agents making competitive decisions on water consumption and trading allocations.
- No specific companies are mentioned in the paper.
- Quantitative figures are not explicitly stated in the abstract.
- The supply chain or value chain relationships described involve agents (interpreted as farmers or agricultural districts) trading allocations among themselves or banking them for future periods.
- No technology transitions are explicitly described.

**Can market volumes reveal traders' rationality and a new risk premium?** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.05854)
- The paper proposes a trading strategy model in the optimal Merton framework, allowing for the estimation of the average risk aversion of traders acting on a specific risky asset.
- No specific companies are mentioned in the paper.
- Quantitative figures are not explicitly stated in the abstract.
- No supply chain or value chain relationships are explicitly described.
- No technology transitions are explicitly described.

**The Privacy Subsidy: Kyle's λ under Noise-Perturbed Order-Flow Observation** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.15746)
- The paper derives the unique linear Kyle equilibrium when a committed Bayesian market maker observes order flow perturbed by independent Gaussian privacy noise.
- No specific companies are mentioned in the paper, but the result is applicable to shielded AMMs (Automated Market Makers) with explicit additive-noise injection.
- Quantitative figures are not explicitly stated in the abstract, but the price-impact coefficient and informed-trader strategy are mentioned to rescale by a single factor in the privacy parameter.
- The supply chain or value chain relationship described involves a market maker observing order flow and making pricing decisions.
- The technology transition described involves the use of noise-perturbed order-flow observation in privacy-preserving cryptocurrency exchanges.

**The Privacy Subsidy in Glosten-Milgrom: Bid-Ask Spread and Welfare under Flip-Noise Direction Observation** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.19742)
- The paper derives a closed-form bid-ask spread and welfare decomposition for the Glosten-Milgrom sequential-trading model under a binary flip channel of probability η.
- No specific companies are mentioned in the paper, but the result is applicable to MPC-based (Multi-Party Computation) matching engines with ε-differentially-private direction disclosure.
- Quantitative figures mentioned include the equilibrium spread μ(1-2η)Δ, where μ is the informed-trader fraction and Δ = v_H - v_L is the value range.
- The supply chain or value chain relationship described involves a market maker observing the trade direction and making pricing decisions.
- The technology transition described involves the use of binary flip channels in privacy mechanisms acting on the direction signal.

**The Privacy Subsidy in Continuous-Time Kyle: Cumulative Welfare under Noise-Perturbed Order-Flow Observation** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25631)
- The paper extends the closed-form privacy-subsidy result from the single-period Kyle model to continuous-time, analyzing a committed Bayesian automated market maker under noise-perturbed order-flow observation.
- No specific companies are mentioned in the paper.
- Quantitative figures mentioned include the price-impact coefficient λ = σ_v / √(σ_u^2 + σ_ε^2) and the cumulative expected transfer |Π_M| = σ_v σ_ε^2 / √(σ_u^2 + σ_ε^2).
- The supply chain or value chain relationship described involves a market maker observing the aggregate order flow and making pricing decisions.
- The technology transition described involves the use of noise-perturbed order-flow observation in privacy-preserving cryptocurrency exchanges.

Arxiv \u2013 Statistical Finance (q-fin..text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers investigate transformer-based models for stock price prediction, examining pre-training strategies on the Toronto Stock Exchange Index.
- A study combines long-memory estimation, rough-volatility diagnostics, and forecasting regressions to examine persistence measures in equity volatility modeling.
- A multi-modal deep learning approach is proposed to predict stock price direction on earnings announcement days, utilizing news sentiment, firm fundamentals, and market dynamics.

**Details:**
**From Index to Equity: Pre-Training Transformers for Stock Return Prediction** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23962)
- The research aims to improve stock price prediction using transformer-based models and pre-training strategies.
- A transformer model was pre-trained on the Toronto Stock Exchange Index (TSX) to predict intra-day return direction.
- The model was fine-tuned on individual TSX stocks and adapted for return-value regression tasks.
- Performance was benchmarked against Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and XGBoost models.
- Pre-training on the market index improved the binary cross-entropy loss for individual stock prediction from 0.69 to 0.64.
- The fine-tuned transformer regression model achieved lower mean squared error than the benchmark models.

**Memory, Roughness, and Information Persistence in Financial Markets: A Structural Approach to Volatility Forecasting** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24285)
- The study examines the joint role of long-memory dynamics, rough-volatility behavior, and persistence-based forecasting features in equity volatility modeling.
- A panel of 115 S&P500 constituents from November 2001 through April 2026 was used.
- The cross-sectional mean Geweke-Porter-Hudak estimate of the memory parameter is $\hat{d} = 0.226$, while the corresponding local-Whittle estimate is $\hat{d} = 0.440$.
- Incorporating cross-sectional persistence aggregates, sectoral persistence measures, and persistence-by-stress interaction terms produces moderate but statistically significant forecasting improvements.
- Forecast gains are strongest during periods of elevated market volatility and in volatility-managed portfolio applications.

**Predicting Stock Price Direction on Earnings Announcement Days using Multi-modal Deep Learning** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25894)
- The study evaluates whether pre-announcement news sentiment, firm fundamentals, and recent market dynamics jointly predict the directional price movement of equities on EA days.
- A multi-modal feature space combines 15 fundamental metrics, 3 price-based technical indicators, and sentiment scores derived from financial news articles processed using FinBERT.
- A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network and a Transformer-based architecture are compared against a logistic regression baseline.
- The Transformer model exhibits superior sensitivity in identifying volatile movements, achieving a higher macro F1-score.
- Ablation experiments show a consistent benefit from incorporating news sentiment.

Arxiv \u2013 Computational Finance (q-fi.text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers investigate nonlinear predictability in transition-energy financial markets, developing a hybrid forecasting framework that combines Student-t Vector Autoregressions with nonlinear recurrent residual learning architectures.
- A quantum PDE framework is developed for European option pricing under local-volatility Black--Scholes and Heston models, offering polynomial improvement factors over grid-based finite-difference baselines.
- Deep Least Squares Monte Carlo methods are proposed for the valuation of variable annuities with guarantees, allowing for the pricing of general variable annuities without simplifying assumptions.
- A faster monotone implied volatility solver, ThiopheneIV, is presented, providing a Black-Scholes implied-volatility solver with a monotone core and explicit production guards.

**Details:**
**Nonlinear and Heavy-Tailed Predictability in Transition-Energy Financial Markets** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26890)
- The paper investigates nonlinear predictability in transition-energy financial markets using a hybrid forecasting framework.
- The framework combines Student-t Vector Autoregressions with nonlinear recurrent residual learning architectures.
- The empirical analysis considers six major exchange-traded funds representing broad equity markets and key transition-sensitive sectors.
- Quantitative figures: excess kurtosis, volatility clustering, and remaining nonlinear dependence after econometric filtering.
- The proposed framework consistently improves predictive accuracy relative to conventional VAR models, with forecasting gains more pronounced during periods of macro-financial stress.

**End-to-End PDE-Based Quantum Algorithms for Multi-Asset Option Pricing under Local and Stochastic Volatility** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26610)
- The paper develops an end-to-end quantum PDE framework for European option pricing under local-volatility Black--Scholes and Heston models.
- The framework takes classical contract and model data as input and returns classical estimates of selected option values.
- Quantitative figures: for $N=2^n$ grid points per spatial direction and $d$ assets, the end-to-end gate complexity has leading grid-size dependence $\widetilde{O}(d^2 N^{2+d/2})$ for local-volatility Black--Scholes and $\widetilde{O}(d^2 N^{d+2})$ for Heston.
- Polynomial improvement factors: $N^{d/2}$ and $N^d$ over grid-based finite-difference baselines.

**Deep Least Squares Monte Carlo methods for the valuation of variable annuities with guarantees** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27182)
- The paper presents modifications of the Least-Squares Monte Carlo (LSMC) algorithm for pricing variable annuities with guarantees.
- The proposed method allows for the pricing of general variable annuities without simplifying assumptions.
- Numerical illustrations: pricing of variable annuities with guaranteed minimum withdrawal benefit under optimal withdrawal strategies, with and without stochastic interest rates.
- Quantitative figures: standard deviation of the estimator increases greatly when interest rates are made stochastic for classical polynomial LSMC.

**Faster Monotone Implied Volatility Solver** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.22427)
- The paper presents ThiopheneIV, a Black-Scholes implied-volatility solver with a monotone core and explicit production guards.
- The solver starts from the simple Choi-Huh-Su L3 lower-bound seed and applies three Euler-Chebyshev steps on a lower branch and three Halley steps on the remaining upper branch.
- Quantitative figures: low latency, close agreement with multiprecision Black reference prices.
- Comparison with recent solvers: J\"ackel's Let's Be Rational.

Arxiv \u2013 Portfolio Management (q-fin.text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers propose a unified quadratic framework for analyzing ownership concentration, overlap, and dependence in a normalized investor-stock matrix.
- A deep learning framework is presented for smarter portfolio decisions by combining LSTM networks, Graph Attention Networks, and sentiment analysis of financial news.
- A study addresses the construction of carbon-sensitive investment funds and hedging strategies for green unit-linked life insurance policies.

**Details:**
**A Unified Theory of Ownership Concentration, Overlap, and Dependence** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26740)
- The paper develops a unified quadratic framework for analyzing ownership concentration, overlap, and dependence.
- No specific companies are mentioned in the paper.
- Quantitative figures include the raw micro concentration $M(A) = \sum_{i,j} A_{ij}^2$ and the benchmark-adjusted dependence $\mathcal{X}(A) = \sum_{i,j} (A_{ij} - p_i s_j)^2 / (p_i s_j)$.
- The paper proves a multiscale aggregation law and introduces a feasible-range sparsity score.
- No specific supply chain or value chain relationships are described.
- The paper focuses on mathematical frameworks and does not describe specific technology transitions.

**From Headlines to Holdings: Deep Learning for Smarter Portfolio Decisions** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.24144)
- The paper presents an end-to-end deep learning framework for portfolio optimization.
- No specific companies are mentioned in the paper, but the model is evaluated on nine U.S. stocks spanning six sectors.
- The framework combines Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, Graph Attention Networks (GAT), and sentiment analysis of financial news.
- Quantitative figures include higher cumulative returns and Sharpe ratios compared to equal-weighted and CAPM-based MVO benchmarks.
- The paper does not describe specific supply chain or value chain relationships.
- The framework unifies multiple elements in a single pipeline, representing a technology transition in portfolio optimization.

**Carbon-Sensitive Fund Construction and Hedging for Green Unit-Linked Life Insurance** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.05676)
- The paper studies the problem of hedging unit-linked life insurance policies with environmental criteria in the investment fund selection process.
- No specific companies are mentioned in the paper.
- The paper designs a portfolio selection rule driven by firms' carbon intensity and tests its effectiveness using real market data.
- Quantitative figures are not explicitly stated, but the paper mentions a numerical analysis to assess the performance of the hedging strategy.
- The paper does not describe specific supply chain or value chain relationships.
- The study addresses the construction of carbon-sensitive investment funds, representing a technology transition in investment fund selection.
